textByHashes.7d7814e5286eef565b5a6ee5dd29e224c3e4b721: I'm sorry, but I can't really help you right now. Perhaps you should ask me again later, or try a different camel.
textByHashes.c07bced09b02baeb2689815708b062d6dd5f6ee4: Well, just you close your eyes first. I'm not doing it while you're watching me!
textByHashes.68357b5792c5ae00fee22c5c8a2c6e925b46fd55: I hope that's what you wanted!
textByHashes.7f26bc09b1686b818101c4ab9986e536fe3458dc: Sorry, were you saying something to me?
textByHashes.434f2b8c41cae78ccd3ddeaf78674be6d560a0dd: Never mind, it is unimportant when I have such important matters weighing on my soul.
textByHashes.021bddf9d992ce39017fffa1d29fc351f14c9ffb: Well, you see, there is a camel called Elly. A beautiful, wondrous camel, with hide like spun gold and teeth that shine like an oasis.
textByHashes.1163979e57c1f335a9499aa5bfa661fcc555de74: I've written many poems describing her beauty. Would you like to hear one?
textByHashes.fd4ba39a29332c8da5979a850990f92b2306454e: Shall I compare thee to a desert's day? Thou art drier and more rough-skinned.
textByHashes.b54dc1e88a1b5039cf9b655563ecd40fe8217d9a: Rough sandstorms shake the cactuses away And summer's heat defers to autumn wind.
textByHashes.15b44ae07aa2926cce82caf0b292a201f765edca: Sometimes too hot the eye of heaven shines, With Guthix' gold complexion often dimmed;
textByHashes.089742a6b6ddd5adf1e6d5d3c1b2d43d3ddd2efd: And every fair from fair sometime declines, By chance or desert's changing course untrimmed...
textByHashes.a529c0ffec773c484c6a9f197b247451fb790074: But I've only got six lines left!
textByHashes.abf8d798003aaa05d3074851f70c8fdb0680274a: Very well. Come back and talk to me if you want to hear more.
textByHashes.a9bbad5f6f08287171aa703ba9e09cad3dedb3f0: Oh, it's you again. Have you come back to listen to my poems?
textByHashes.055e8a95961c19430ce8a57284c6bd6595bf70c5: That's so kind of you. Which one would you like to hear?
textByHashes.2592be32af7bd492610462e88eb63144fa35427f: 'Shall I compare thee to a desert's day' is my finest yet, but I've also composed others.
textByHashes.5b0bceda4eb6628b3706521dd87219380fcf3037: But thine eternal desert shall not fade Nor lose possession of that sand thou owest;
textByHashes.efa9cd961cadcb8d832708dd148c30280cbb4242: Nor Zamorak brag thou art in his shades, When in eternal lines to sand thou growest,
textByHashes.90e7bead425241b91648e61ddfe5d6562594723f: So long as camels breathe or eyes can see, So long lives this, and this gives life to thee.
textByHashes.9611eba4caa98b31a6118fa50140f34de1a29d48: Ah, Elly, how beautiful you are.
textByHashes.6fafd79674d9dd0222715b8e33d3a41daa4aeda3: I suspect she loves another - Ollie, another camel who roams with her to the north.
textByHashes.af889386d8b7e4b971d2617c7cbfd40dd4ebe4dd: So I shall stay here and compose poems for her.
textByHashes.0c8ec8f1bbacc8ceea5e17ee5b99fa9e7339d538: She seems happy enough as she is, and I have my poems to comfort me.
textByHashes.2da603dc59d3d9bb83e24743fbe109b6c272b493: If you want to hear my poems once more, please come back again.
textByHashes.130fbf120ac3cf5017c1f413a5e081b3423af2ff: I wrote this poem when I went to the oasis to nibble at a tree, then discovered I'd left nothing for Elly to nibble. I was distraught.
textByHashes.7e42163cd6395c71b0e4244d505cf37d51bb9f71: I have nibbled the cacti that were by the oasis,
textByHashes.abebe1e38e9fc154ce0b7d1dc979f84771091c9c: and which you were probably saving for lunch.
textByHashes.fe435f1570f52e64efd88e25a893167fe9b7bd2c: Forgive me, they were delicious, so crunchy and so cold.
textByHashes.56807e3d7ed451a772af1ffc23e3b10bf4e3f3b7: I wonder if she's forgiven me for eating her snack.
textByHashes.62d0ae0fdb5f8db856b0ad4d9f45adbbe8d9f1b9: Ah, well. I shall return to writing poems to Elly's beauty.
